title: Living in Boulder vlog 11 -  16 -  2017: French Castles on Google Earth

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his late-night session cataloging French castles on Google Earth using Flickr, working through them alphabetically. He also discusses reading Plato's Laws and its detailed vision for a utopian city-state of 5,040 people, which he wants to use as inspiration for designing his own version centered on Boulder. He shares excitement about eventually making immersive videos narrating castle histories.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: finds them aesthetically beautiful and exciting to explore, especially their hilltop locations
fascinated by its detailed city-state design; wants to apply similar thinking to create his own 'little world' modeled on Boulder
wants to get high and narrate castle histories to create an immersive past-life-like experience
feels the Boulder Google Earth project is the closest to done; using the castle goal as motivation to keep going

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder Vlog for November 16th, because right now it's 3.37 a.m. on November 17th.
So, today I think I got up kind of late, and I was working on castles and listening to Plato laws on my website,

And because he wanted to design everything about it, like all of the holidays and the inner workings and laws
And even the population, he wanted to be 5,440 people.
So, I was listening to that. I want to listen to that over and over again because, yeah, I can't remember that many of the details right now,
But because I want to do that for Boulder Vlog, make my own, you know, little world.
And, you know, he references, of course, all these other different city-states too, like one of them is the Spartans.
All the men between the ages of 25 and 30 would go, every other year would go tour all of the last city in the Spartan country.
So, yeah, that's been pretty interesting.
And so, yeah, I'm doing castles. Right now it's France, which has the most castles out of all of them.
And then after France I'll do Germany because I'm going down alphabetically, which has this, like, the second most.
France is like 300 and something.

So I'll do that from scratch.

And a lot of the times it's not the same spelling as, so I have to, like, research and find it.
Um, and on Wikipedia, sometimes it's not on English Wikipedia, I have to go to French Wikipedia.
But, uh, they're all 3D'd.
Like, they've got these little cartoon models of all of them, um, if you put it on 3D buildings.

So, I'm not going to, but.
But, so, I want to finish, uh, Castles and Confugals before I bounce back to, um, Google Earth and Run because that's the thing that I'm the closest to finishing.
Actually, the thing that I'm closest to finishing is Boulder.
Google Earth and Boulder.
